mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5 percent, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, depressing equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins and triggers equity sell-offs within a week.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict SPX direction in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle, a persistent rise in the unemployment rate acts as a lagging indicator of peak corporate earnings, triggering a mean-reversion decline in equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in tightening periods do not exhibit statistically significant negative skew compared to easing periods (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP directly scales corporate revenue expectations, producing a linear positive relationship with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon as earnings revisions propagate.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in GDP growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 110, it signals capacity utilization stress that compresses operating margins and forces equity de-rating over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict SPX direction in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous rise in payroll employment and federal funds rate indicates an overheating economy where wage-push inflation forces aggressive central bank action, ultimately triggering a liquidity-driven equity correction within a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict SPX direction in more than 55% of out-of-sample instances over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: In a disinflationary environment, rising consumer credit balances reflect resilient household demand that supports corporate earnings and lifts equity valuations over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns in disinflationary periods do not exhibit statistically significant positive skew compared to inflationary periods (p > 0.05).
